name: Yankee Trace
about: The Golf Club at Yankee Trace is a 27-hole championship municipal golf course located in Centerville, Ohio. Opened in 1995 with an original 18 holes, the facility was expanded to 27 holes by designer Gene Bates in 2002. It is recognized as a top 50 municipal course in the country and a top 5 public course in Ohio. The venue offers traditional golf on a championship course, as well as a practice facility and golf simulators, making it suitable for both on-course play and off-season training. Memberships are available for 9, 18, and 27-hole access.
why_golfers_like_it: Recognized as a top 50 municipal and top 5 public course in Ohio
27 holes of championship golf designed by Gene Bates
Combines traditional play with modern simulator technology for year-round access

food_and_drink.notes: Full restaurant service available at 'The Grille at Yankee Trace' with seasonal brunches, fish fries, and special event dining
Full bar service available at the on-site restaurant

simulator_hardware.notes: Specific simulator hardware models are not listed on the website

faq.question: Do I need clubs to use the simulators?
faq.answer: The website does not specify, but as a golf facility, it is highly recommended that you bring your own clubs for the simulator experience.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drink?
faq.answer: While food and drink are available on-site at 'The Grille at Yankee Trace', the website does not specify if outside food and drink are permitted in the simulator area.

faq.question: How long does it typically take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: The website does not provide specific timing for simulator rounds, but as a full golf course, typical 18-hole rounds take between 4-5 hours depending on pace of play.

faq.question: Is this venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, the venue offers junior/youth golf instruction programs, making it a family-friendly option for golfers of all ages and skill levels.
